judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are comparable on divisions/ordering/F-06 judgment call, but Report 1 correctly identifies the RESULTS_DB shared-path collision risk and mandates a distinct RESULTS_DB per engineer, while Report 2 explicitly (and incorrectly) dismisses it as "harmless as-is," missing expectation (d).

report_1.evidence: Engineer B — pipeline/registry.py: F-02 (aurora-1 mapping — see caveat in Q3), F-05 (DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION → v3), F-09 (DEFAULT_DATASET → eval_set.jsonl). Three independent one-line config edits in one file.

report_2.evidence: Lane B — pipeline/registry.py (one engineer): F-02, F-05, F-09. Three one-line constant/dict edits in an 11-line file. Trivial but must go to a single owner — three people editing the same tiny file would conflict for no benefit.

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 (fully parallel, no code collisions): Engineer B's config edits (F-02*/F-05/F-09), Engineer D's prompt edits (F-03/F-10), and the failing tests first — F-11 (Engineer C) and F-12 (Engineer A).

report_2.evidence: Lanes A, B, C, D run fully in parallel in the first wave — they touch disjoint files, so there's no cross-lane conflict... Lane C — pipeline/rubric.py + rubric test (one engineer): F-01 then F-11.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — do not assign as a blind code edit. "Tighten PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS so borderline answers score lower" changes user-facing scoring output, and the finding gives no target numbers... escalate for the intended thresholds first, then implement; don't let an engineer pick numbers.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — a judgment call, not a mechanical fix... has no target values and silently recalibrates every score the pipeline produces, breaking comparability with past runs. This needs the eval/rubric owner to pick and sign off on the new numbers... it shouldn't be dropped on an engineer to guess.

report_1.evidence: Shared test resource. tests/conftest.py uses RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). Parallel test runs from different worktrees would clobber a single shared DB. Mitigation: each engineer sets a distinct RESULTS_DB (project documentation: isolated test resource per batch).

report_2.evidence: The RESULTS_DB env var referenced in tests/conftest.py (defaults to /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db); harmless as-is but worth knowing exists.
